prompt: If the completed column for $(P \wedge Q)$ is `T F F F`, which column correctly gives $\sim (P \wedge Q)$?
explanation: The outer operator is negation, so each truth value in the finished inner column must be flipped row by row.

statement: To evaluate an outer expression, use the completed inner column directly.
explanation: Outer evaluation is built from the finished inner result. That is how truth-table construction moves outward through the formula.

prompt: If row 2 of $(P \wedge Q)$ is F, then row 2 of $\sim (P \wedge Q)$ is ___.
explanation: Negation flips the value in the same row. An F in the inner column becomes T in the outer negated column.

prompt: Classify each step for the formula $\sim (P \wedge Q) \vee R$.

items.text: After $(P \wedge Q)$, compute the final disjunction immediately.
Use the completed column for $(P \wedge Q)$ to build the negated column.
Rearrange the rows before computing $\sim (P \wedge Q)$.

explanation: Truth-table construction follows the syntax outward one level at a time. The next step after an inner expression is the immediate outer expression, with row order unchanged.

prompt: Arrange the evaluation steps for $\sim (P \wedge Q) \vee R$ after the base columns are already filled.
items: Compute the final column for $\sim (P \wedge Q) \vee R$.
explanation: The formula must be evaluated from the inside out. Each outer step uses the result of the step just before it.
